strengths: Targeted and varied interview prompts that generate actionable user insights
Effective self-management using Asana and digital reminders
Creative thinking evidenced by iterative prototyping with three distinct sketches
Strong information-literacy through diverse sources and clear linkage to sub-questions
weaknesses: Resource list missing estimated collection dates and collaborator assignments
Final product photo lacks annotations tying features back to success criteria
Research-question rationale is incomplete, weakening its connection to the learning goal
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **The Applying Skills section showcases excellent use of ATL skills**: self-management tools keep the workflow on track, thoughtful interview design yields valuable insights, creative prototyping guides design decisions, and research is both diverse and purpose-driven. To deepen this work, specify **when and with whom** each resource will be collected, annotate your final product to highlight how it meets defined criteria, and complete the rationale for each research question to clearly demonstrate how it advances your learning goal.

strengths: Clear connection between the learning goal and the studentâs personal experience
Comprehensive project plan outlining tasks and activities
weaknesses: Short-term goals lack dates and sequencing
Justification column is overly lengthy and unfocused
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **The planning section demonstrates a strong alignment between the learnerâs goal and their personal context, and offers a detailed breakdown of tasks.** However, without **measurable success criteria**, clear **deadlines and sequencing**, or a populated **rubric table**, it is difficult to assess progress or feasibility. Additionally, the overly long rationales dilute focus. Refining each justification to one or two sentences, specifying quantifiable indicators (for example, âgrow at least three healthy spinach plants in four weeksâ), and assigning deadlines will make the plan more actionable and assessable.

strengths: Thorough use of the CRAAP method to evaluate research sources
Insightful reflection on self-management gains and reduced stress
Strong environmental impact analysis linking local sourcing to sustainability
Meaningful reflection on skill growth in painting and art, tied to future community workshops
Authentic user-feedback interpretation with real-world application
weaknesses: Aesthetics evaluation lacks quantitative audience feedback for support
Materials critique identifies issues but needs proposed testing of alternatives
Mind-map of improvements is broad; top priorities are not clearly indicated
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **The Reflection section is richly detailed**, combining rigorous source evaluation, personal growth insights, sustainability connections, and real user feedback. These elements illustrate both cognitive and affective learning impacts. To strengthen your reflections, incorporate **quantitative data** (such as survey ratings) into your aesthetics appraisal, propose **specific tests** of alternative materials (for example, larger bottles or modular connectors), and **prioritize the two most critical improvements** from your mind-map for your next prototype cycle.

annotations.comment: Your interview prompts are targeted and varied. They will generate the user insights essential for refining your prototype.
Excellent use of Asana and digital reminders to organise your workflow. This shows strong self-management in meeting project milestones.
Your resource list is clear. Add estimated collection dates and collaborators for each item to strengthen your production plan.
You show strong creative thinking by iterating three distinct sketches. This iterative prototyping supports informed design decisions.
Your final product photo clearly shows completion. Annotate or reflect on two features that best match your success criteria to underline the designâs effectiveness.
Great integration of the CRAAP evaluation method. You clearly improved your humanities research by assessing currency, authority, and purpose of each source.
Strong reflection on self-management gains (Gantt, reminders). You link these tools to reduced stress and on-track DIP performanceâexcellent reflection on learning impact.
Strong environmental impact reflection, linking local sourcing to reduced food miles and crop carbon-sink benefits. Excellent connection to sustainability goals.
Insightful reflection on how developing painting and art skills increased your confidence. Linking these skills to future community workshops is strong self-growth evidence.
Your aesthetics evaluation is thorough. To strengthen evidence, add target-audience survey ratings (e.g., â80% rated it highly visually appealingâ) for quantitative support.
Authentic user feedback email shows real-world impact and satisfaction. Reflect on how this prompts you to plan targeted iterations (e.g., bottle size adjustment).
Your research questions are well chosen. Complete the rationale column by explicitly noting how each question advances your learning goal.
Good materials critique. You note bottle size limits yields; next iteration test larger bottles or modular connectors to fully meet your planâs sufficiency criteria.

Your learning goal is clearly connected to personal experience, but you havenât defined measurable success criteria. Add specific indicators (e.g. âgrow at least three healthy spinach plants in four weeksâ) to make your goal assessable.
Short-term goals list activities but lack dates and sequencing. Include deadlines and responsible persons to strengthen your planâs feasibility.
Your success-criteria rubric table is empty. Populate it with concise, product-focused criteria that align to your learning goal and intended product.
The project plan is detailed but your justification column is very lengthy. Condense each rationale into one or two sentences to maintain clarity and focus.
You demonstrate strong information-literacy skills through diverse research (videos, e-books, experts). Linking each source to your sub-questions shows purposeful inquiry.
Your mind-map of improvements is comprehensive. Prioritise the top two changes in your next prototype to focus resources and test effectiveness efficiently.
